title: teucrium polium subsp. gnaphalodes
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. gnaphalodes, also known as a subspecies of the shrubby germander plant, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Teucrium polium subsp. gnaphalodes include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
